content.problemStatement: Create a program to calculate and display the total length of two specified words: **Naruto** and **Sasuke**.
content.inputFormat: The input may contain a single integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100) denoting the number of test cases. If the input is empty, assume T = 1. No further data is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of the lengths of the strings "Naruto" and "Sasuke" (which is always 12).

sampleTestCases.explanation: No input means a single test case is assumed. The sum of the lengths of "Naruto" (6) and "Sasuke" (6) is 12.
T = 3, so the answer 12 is printed three times, once per test case.

hidden.label: edge case – single test case with whitespace

solution.approach: The lengths of the two fixed strings are known: |Naruto| = 6 and |Sasuke| = 6. Their sum is 12. For each test case we simply print 12. The algorithm runs in O(T) time, where T is the number of test cases, and uses O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
